title: Configurable Enrollment Window Per Life Event Type
description: The system shall maintain a configurable enrollment window for each qualifying life event type. A single hardcoded 30-day window must not be applied uniformly across all event types.

title: Support Calendar-Day and Business-Day Windows
description: The system shall support both calendar-day and business-day enrollment windows, configurable per event type.

title: Submission Deadline Calculation
description: The system shall calculate the submission deadline as: Event Date (Day 0) + Configured Window Days. The employee has through 11:59 PM on the final day to submit the election change. Example: Marriage May 15 + 30 days = deadline June 14.
dayZeroRule: Event date is Day 0. Employee has through 11:59 PM on the 30th calendar day following the event to submit.

title: Late Submission Handling
description: The system shall prevent automatic coverage changes for late submissions. Late submissions (submitted after the window closes) shall be routed to HR exception review. The system shall not automatically update coverage from a late submission.

title: Event-Specific Coverage Effective-Date Rules
description: The system shall apply event-specific effective-date rules. Coverage effective dates differ by event type: Birth = birth date; Marriage = event date or first of following month (needs confirmation); Loss of Coverage = day after loss. The system shall not apply a single universal effective-date rule.

title: Permitted Election Changes Per Event Type
description: The system shall restrict permitted enrollment changes to those defined for each event type. A Birth event permits adding a child but does not permit adding a spouse. A Marriage event permits adding a spouse. The system shall enforce these restrictions.

title: Independent Date Tracking: Event, Submission, Deadline, Effective
description: The system shall track event date, submission date, submission deadline, and coverage effective date independently as separate fields. These four dates may differ and must not be collapsed into a single field.

dataModel.eventDate: date — when the qualifying life event occurred
dataModel.submissionDate: date | null — when the employee submitted the election
dataModel.coverageEffectiveDate: date — when coverage change takes effect

title: Documentation Requirements Per Event Type
description: The system shall track and enforce documentation requirements per life event type. Marriage requires marriage certificate; Birth requires birth certificate; Adoption requires placement agreement and adoption decree; Divorce requires divorce decree. Documentation status shall gate final approval.

title: HR Approval for Exceptions and Late Submissions
description: The system shall route late submissions and flagged life events to HR for exception review and approval. HR approval shall be tracked with timestamp and approver ID. Approved exceptions shall proceed to coverage update; rejected exceptions shall close the event.

title: Life Event Audit Trail
description: The system shall maintain an immutable audit trail for all life event changes, including event type, event date, submission date, election changes made, coverage effective date, documentation received, approvals, and final status.

title: AI Flag: Missing Enrollment Window Rule
description: The system shall flag life event types that do not have a defined enrollment window or effective-date rule. The AI Requirements Agent shall surface these as open decisions requiring business owner confirmation, not silently apply a default.

demoAIInsight.recommendation: Create event-specific enrollment rules rather than applying the marriage 30-day window universally. Requires Benefits/Legal confirmation.

title: No Universal Default Enrollment Window
description: The system shall not assume that all qualifying life events share the same enrollment window. Event rules marked REQUIRES_CONFIRMATION shall display a provisional label in the UI ('Provisional — Requires Benefits/Legal Confirmation') until approved.
